dimensions.gap: Kitex and gRPC-Go still have stronger latency-critical RPC evidence. gofly has unary and stream benchmark coverage, but RPC latency budgets are not yet promoted into a ratcheted blocking policy.
dimensions.todo: Add an RPC latency ratchet policy tied to unary and stream benchmark trend confidence, keeping weak-signal rows report-only.

dimensions.gap: go-zero and Kratos remain clearer for long-term scaffold migration expectations. gofly has compatibility fixtures and migration examples, but lacks one adopter-facing fidelity matrix tying regeneration diffs, rollback notes, and framework-specific smoke gates together.
dimensions.todo: Create a migration fidelity matrix for Gin, go-zero, Kratos, and Kitex paths with deterministic regeneration, diff categories, rollback notes, and gate commands.

dimensions.gap: Kratos and go-zero are still easier to trust for production deployment conventions. gofly renders Helm and Kustomize assets, but policy conformance and fallback reporting are not yet captured as one machine-readable release artifact.
dimensions.todo: Add cloud-native policy conformance evidence for Helm/Kustomize profiles, NetworkPolicy, HPA, PDB, ServiceMonitor, kubeconform fallback status, and rollout gates.

dimensions.gap: Mature frameworks often make failure recovery obvious through stable diagnostics and common troubleshooting paths. gofly has doctor and release JSON contracts, but support-bundle evidence and generated failure reports need to be indexed as a product surface.
dimensions.todo: Productize support bundle evidence with doctor/release JSON next actions, generated project failure reports, and stable CLI JSON contract tests.

dimensions.gap: gofly has stronger governance automation than many frameworks, but the dashboard is still a generated report rather than a polished adopter-facing contract that summarizes release readiness, API tiers, security, coverage, benchmark ratchets, and task status.
dimensions.todo: Define a dashboard contract with stable summary fields for release readiness, API tiers, benchmark ratchet status, coverage, security, and aiflow task status.
